truncation.content:
SHELL := /bin/bash
DC = docker compose -f docker-compose.yml
PYTHON ?= python3

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark variables — all can be overridden on the command line
# ==============================================================================
#
# Auth (auto-read from .env, no manual token needed in normal use):
#   AUTH_TOKEN              pre-obtained JWT (optional — login is automatic)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    admin e-mail  (read from .env by default)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD admin password (read from .env by default)
#
# Study scope:
#   SUITE       baselines | ablation | full | all  (default: baselines)
#   DATASET     path to a single ex_*.json; empty = every ex_*.json
#   RUN_NAME    fixed directory name for idempotent / resumable runs
#
# Judge management (for existing runs, no re-creation needed):
#   REJUDGE=1       run only NEW judges not yet in stored results
#   REAGGREGATE=1   recompute scores/kappa offline (no LLM calls)
#   INCLUDE_JUDGES  comma-separated judge names to use in reaggregation
#
# Quick reference:
#   make benchmark-study                          # baselines suite, all datasets
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=ablation           # ablation suite
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=all RUN_NAME=pub   # full study, resumable
#   make benchmark-study DATASET=evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json RUN_NAME=smoke
#   make benchmark-comparative RUN_NAME=smoke     # single-dataset, host-side
#   make benchmark-rejudge    RUN_NAME=smoke       # add new judges to existing run
#   make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=smoke      # offline score recompute
#   make benchmark-report     RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/smoke

BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR    ?= evaluation/results
BENCHMARK_INPUT         ?= evaluation/datasets/research_input.json
SUITE                   ?= baselines
RUN_NAME                ?=
DATASET                 ?=
REJUDGE                 ?=
REAGGREGATE             ?=
ONLY_EVALUATE           ?=
ONLY_JUDGE              ?=
SKIP_EVALUATE           ?=
FORCE_JUDGE             ?=
FORCE_CREATE            ?=
INCLUDE_JUDGES          ?=
# Host user identity — passed to docker exec so result files are user-owned
HOST_UID                := $(shell id -u)
HOST_GID                := $(shell id -g)
SCORPION_CONFIG_ID      ?= 925b3c1b-df6f-4a63-89e5-8c3bcfdc3e3f
ORCHESTRATOR_URL        ?= http://localhost:12013/api/v1
CONFIG_SERVICE_URL      ?= http://localhost:12011
EVALUATION_URL          ?= http://localhost:12015/api/v1/evaluate
AUTH_BASE_URL           ?= http://localhost:12000
AUTH_TOKEN              ?=
# Auto-read credentials from .env — no manual input required
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)

# Legacy aliases (keep old invocations working)
CONFIGURATION_ID        ?= $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
METAMODEL_ID            ?=
BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT ?= evaluation/results/research_created_projects.json
BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_evaluations.json
BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_full_run.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT  ?= evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR ?= evaluation/results

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target strings by suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_T_FULL      := FullSystem:$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
_T_BASELINES := Baseline-Direct:direct_llm,Baseline-ReAct:react_agent,Baseline-CodeGen:code_gen
_T_ABLATION  := Ablation-NoMetamodel:ablation:no_metamodel_guide,Ablation-NoBlackboard:ablation:no_blackboard,Ablation-NoRAG:ablation:no_rag
_T_MODIFY    := ModifyVsRegen:modify

ifeq ($(SUITE),baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),full)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),modify)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),smoke)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),all)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES),$(_T_ABLATION),$(_T_MODIFY)
else
$(error Unknown SUITE=$(SUITE). Use: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all)
endif

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared auth macro — acquires a JWT and exports it as $$TOKEN.
# Uses AUTH_TOKEN directly if provided; otherwise logs in from .env credentials.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
define _GET_TOKEN
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	if [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" ] || [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL or BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD not set in .env"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Attempting auth for $(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)..."; \
	RESPONSE=$$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12010/api/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}" \
		|| curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12000/api/v1/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}"); \
	TOKEN=$$(echo "$$RESPONSE" | $(PYTHON) -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access', ''))" 2>/dev/null); \
	if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: Auth failed. Response from server:"; \
		echo "$$RESPONSE"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Auth OK"; \
fi
endef

# Same macro for inside a running Docker container (uses internal hostnames)
define _GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	TOKEN=$$(docker exec benchmark-env python3 -c \
		"import httpx; r=httpx.post('http://auth-service:12010/api/auth/login/', \
		    json={'email':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)','password':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)'}, \
		    timeout=20); r.raise_for_status(); print(r.json()['access'])"); \
	echo "  Auth OK inside container ($(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL))"; \
fi
endef

# Webapp-only services (auth + config + frontend — no orchestrator/artifacts)
WEBAPP_SERVICES = traefik auth-postgres auth-service config-mongo config-service webapp

.PHONY: help up up-webapp down down-webapp logs logs-webapp status clean \
        benchmark-study benchmark-comparative \
        benchmark-rejudge benchmark-reaggregate benchmark-judge \
        benchmark-report benchmark-auth-check \
        benchmark-create benchmark-evaluate benchmark-run-all \
        benchmark-baselines benchmark-run-server benchmark-run-unified

help:
	@echo "Co-Creation Framework"
	@echo "====================="
	@echo ""
	@echo "Stack management"
	@echo "----------------"
	@echo "  make up             Start full stack (all services)"
	@echo "  make up-webapp      Start minimal stack (traefik + auth + config + webapp)"
	@echo "  make down           Stop all services"
	@echo "  make logs           Tail all service logs"
	@echo "  make status         Show service status"
	@echo "  make clean          Stop all services and remove volumes"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Research benchmarks"
	@echo "-------------------"
	@echo "  make benchmark-study              Full study inside Docker (idempotent)."
	@echo "                                    Loops over all ex_*.json datasets."
	@echo "    SUITE=baselines|ablation|modify|smoke|full|all  (default: baselines)"
	@echo "    DATASET=path/to/ex_N.json          run a single dataset instead"
	@echo "    RUN_NAME=my_run                    fixed dir name for resumable runs"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-comparative        Single-dataset run on the host."
	@echo "    BENCHMARK_INPUT=...  RUN_NAME=... SUITE=..."
	@echo "    REJUDGE=1            add new judges to an existing run"
	@echo "    REAGGREGATE=1        recompute scores offline (no LLM calls)"
	@echo "    INCLUDE_JUDGES=j1,j2 subset of judges for reaggregation"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-rejudge   RUN_NAME=<run>   (shortcut for REJUDGE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=<run>  (shortcut for REAGGREGATE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-judge     RUN_NAME=<run>   skip creation, run judge on snapshots"
	@echo "  make benchmark-report    RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/<run>"
	@echo "  make benchmark-auth-check                  verify credentials"
	@echo ""
	@echo "SUITE values: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Auth: credentials are auto-read from .env (BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL / PASSWORD)."
	@echo "      Override with  AUTH_TOKEN=<jwt>  if you have a pre-obtained token."

# ==============================================================================
# Stack targets
# ==============================================================================

up:
	$(DC) up -d --build

up-webapp:
	$(DC) up -d --build $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

down:
	$(DC) down

down-webapp:
	$(DC) stop $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

logs:
	$(DC) logs -f

logs-webapp:
	$(DC) logs -f $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

status:
	$(DC) ps

clean:
	$(DC) down -v --remove-orphans

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark: setup and auth check
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-setup:
	@echo "Ensuring benchmarking configuration exists..."
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	curl -s -X GET "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)/api/v1/configurations/$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		-H "Authorization: Bearer $$TOKEN" | grep -q "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" || \
		(echo "  Config $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID) not found. It should be seeded automatically on config-service startup." && \
		 echo "  If you just started the services, wait a few seconds and try again." && \
		 exit 1)
	@echo "  Benchmarking configuration OK"

benchmark-auth-check:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	echo "  Token length: $${#TOKEN}"; \
	echo "  benchmark-auth-check: OK"

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-study — main entry point (runs inside Docker, idempotent)
#
# Starts the benchmark container, then launches run_all_benchmarks.sh inside
# it with the chosen SUITE.  Each dataset gets a stable RUN_NAME so re-running
# the same command resumes from where it left off.
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-study: benchmark-setup
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	echo "Running suite='$(SUITE)' inside container..."; \
	INP="$(DATASET)"; [ -z "$$INP" ] && INP="$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)"; \
	if [ -n "$$INP" ]; then \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/dev/null" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
				--input "$$INP" \
				--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
				--output-dir "/app/src/evaluation/results" \
				$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
				--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
				--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
				--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
				--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
				--token "$$TOKEN" \
				--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
				--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
				--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
				$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
				$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_EVALUATE),--only-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_JUDGE),--only-judge,) \
				$(if $(SKIP_EVALUATE),--skip-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
				$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",); \
	else \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/datasets" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			bash /app/src/evaluation/run_all_benchmarks.sh; \
	fi

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-comparative — single dataset, runs on host (fast iteration)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-comparative: benchmark-setup
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
		$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-rejudge — add new judges to an existing run (no re-creation)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously started run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-rejudge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--rejudge \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-reaggregate — recompute scores/kappa from stored judge data (no LLM)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously evaluated run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-reaggregate:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--reaggregate \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-judge — skip creation, run LLM-as-judge on existing snapshots
# Combines --only-evaluate --rejudge --reaggregate in one step.
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously created run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-judge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--only-judge \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-report — generate markdown/CSV report from an existing run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-report:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_DIR)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_DIR (e.g., evaluation/results/benchmark_ex_01_audit)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparison_reporter "$(RUN_DIR)"

# ==============================================================================
# Legacy / convenience targets (kept for backwards compatibility)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-create:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode create \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-evaluate:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode evaluate \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN"

benchmark-run-all:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode both \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-baselines:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	RUN_TS=$$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); \
	CONSOLE_LOG="$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)/baselines_console_$${RUN_TS}.log"; \
	mkdir -p "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)"; \
	echo "Running baselines on $(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)  (log: $$CONSOLE_LOG)"; \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
			--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
			--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
			--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			--token "$$TOKEN" \
			--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
			--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
	2>&1 | tee "$$CONSOLE_LOG"

benchmark-run-server:
	@echo "Launching server-side benchmark auto-runner (profile: autorun)..."
	@echo "All services must already be up (make up)."
	@echo "Results -> evaluation/results/ (mounted volume)"
	docker compose --profile autorun up --build benchmark-runner

benchmark-run-unified:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \

displayContent.text:
SHELL := /bin/bash
DC = docker compose -f docker-compose.yml
PYTHON ?= python3

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark variables — all can be overridden on the command line
# ==============================================================================
#
# Auth (auto-read from .env, no manual token needed in normal use):
#   AUTH_TOKEN              pre-obtained JWT (optional — login is automatic)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    admin e-mail  (read from .env by default)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD admin password (read from .env by default)
#
# Study scope:
#   SUITE       baselines | ablation | full | all  (default: baselines)
#   DATASET     path to a single ex_*.json; empty = every ex_*.json
#   RUN_NAME    fixed directory name for idempotent / resumable runs
#
# Judge management (for existing runs, no re-creation needed):
#   REJUDGE=1       run only NEW judges not yet in stored results
#   REAGGREGATE=1   recompute scores/kappa offline (no LLM calls)
#   INCLUDE_JUDGES  comma-separated judge names to use in reaggregation
#
# Quick reference:
#   make benchmark-study                          # baselines suite, all datasets
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=ablation           # ablation suite
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=all RUN_NAME=pub   # full study, resumable
#   make benchmark-study DATASET=evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json RUN_NAME=smoke
#   make benchmark-comparative RUN_NAME=smoke     # single-dataset, host-side
#   make benchmark-rejudge    RUN_NAME=smoke       # add new judges to existing run
#   make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=smoke      # offline score recompute
#   make benchmark-report     RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/smoke

BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR    ?= evaluation/results
BENCHMARK_INPUT         ?= evaluation/datasets/research_input.json
SUITE                   ?= baselines
RUN_NAME                ?=
DATASET                 ?=
REJUDGE                 ?=
REAGGREGATE             ?=
ONLY_EVALUATE           ?=
ONLY_JUDGE              ?=
SKIP_EVALUATE           ?=
FORCE_JUDGE             ?=
FORCE_CREATE            ?=
INCLUDE_JUDGES          ?=
# Host user identity — passed to docker exec so result files are user-owned
HOST_UID                := $(shell id -u)
HOST_GID                := $(shell id -g)
SCORPION_CONFIG_ID      ?= 925b3c1b-df6f-4a63-89e5-8c3bcfdc3e3f
ORCHESTRATOR_URL        ?= http://localhost:12013/api/v1
CONFIG_SERVICE_URL      ?= http://localhost:12011
EVALUATION_URL          ?= http://localhost:12015/api/v1/evaluate
AUTH_BASE_URL           ?= http://localhost:12000
AUTH_TOKEN              ?=
# Auto-read credentials from .env — no manual input required
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)

# Legacy aliases (keep old invocations working)
CONFIGURATION_ID        ?= $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
METAMODEL_ID            ?=
BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT ?= evaluation/results/research_created_projects.json
BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_evaluations.json
BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_full_run.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT  ?= evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR ?= evaluation/results

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target strings by suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_T_FULL      := FullSystem:$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
_T_BASELINES := Baseline-Direct:direct_llm,Baseline-ReAct:react_agent,Baseline-CodeGen:code_gen
_T_ABLATION  := Ablation-NoMetamodel:ablation:no_metamodel_guide,Ablation-NoBlackboard:ablation:no_blackboard,Ablation-NoRAG:ablation:no_rag
_T_MODIFY    := ModifyVsRegen:modify

ifeq ($(SUITE),baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),full)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),modify)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),smoke)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),all)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES),$(_T_ABLATION),$(_T_MODIFY)
else
$(error Unknown SUITE=$(SUITE). Use: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all)
endif

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared auth macro — acquires a JWT and exports it as $$TOKEN.
# Uses AUTH_TOKEN directly if provided; otherwise logs in from .env credentials.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
define _GET_TOKEN
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	if [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" ] || [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL or BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD not set in .env"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Attempting auth for $(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)..."; \
	RESPONSE=$$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12010/api/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}" \
		|| curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12000/api/v1/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}"); \
	TOKEN=$$(echo "$$RESPONSE" | $(PYTHON) -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access', ''))" 2>/dev/null); \
	if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: Auth failed. Response from server:"; \
		echo "$$RESPONSE"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Auth OK"; \
fi
endef

# Same macro for inside a running Docker container (uses internal hostnames)
define _GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	TOKEN=$$(docker exec benchmark-env python3 -c \
		"import httpx; r=httpx.post('http://auth-service:12010/api/auth/login/', \
		    json={'email':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)','password':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)'}, \
		    timeout=20); r.raise_for_status(); print(r.json()['access'])"); \
	echo "  Auth OK inside container ($(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL))"; \
fi
endef

# Webapp-only services (auth + config + frontend — no orchestrator/artifacts)
WEBAPP_SERVICES = traefik auth-postgres auth-service config-mongo config-service webapp

.PHONY: help up up-webapp down down-webapp logs logs-webapp status clean \
        benchmark-study benchmark-comparative \
        benchmark-rejudge benchmark-reaggregate benchmark-judge \
        benchmark-report benchmark-auth-check \
        benchmark-create benchmark-evaluate benchmark-run-all \
        benchmark-baselines benchmark-run-server benchmark-run-unified

help:
	@echo "Co-Creation Framework"
	@echo "====================="
	@echo ""
	@echo "Stack management"
	@echo "----------------"
	@echo "  make up             Start full stack (all services)"
	@echo "  make up-webapp      Start minimal stack (traefik + auth + config + webapp)"
	@echo "  make down           Stop all services"
	@echo "  make logs           Tail all service logs"
	@echo "  make status         Show service status"
	@echo "  make clean          Stop all services and remove volumes"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Research benchmarks"
	@echo "-------------------"
	@echo "  make benchmark-study              Full study inside Docker (idempotent)."
	@echo "                                    Loops over all ex_*.json datasets."
	@echo "    SUITE=baselines|ablation|modify|smoke|full|all  (default: baselines)"
	@echo "    DATASET=path/to/ex_N.json          run a single dataset instead"
	@echo "    RUN_NAME=my_run                    fixed dir name for resumable runs"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-comparative        Single-dataset run on the host."
	@echo "    BENCHMARK_INPUT=...  RUN_NAME=... SUITE=..."
	@echo "    REJUDGE=1            add new judges to an existing run"
	@echo "    REAGGREGATE=1        recompute scores offline (no LLM calls)"
	@echo "    INCLUDE_JUDGES=j1,j2 subset of judges for reaggregation"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-rejudge   RUN_NAME=<run>   (shortcut for REJUDGE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=<run>  (shortcut for REAGGREGATE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-judge     RUN_NAME=<run>   skip creation, run judge on snapshots"
	@echo "  make benchmark-report    RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/<run>"
	@echo "  make benchmark-auth-check                  verify credentials"
	@echo ""
	@echo "SUITE values: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Auth: credentials are auto-read from .env (BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL / PASSWORD)."
	@echo "      Override with  AUTH_TOKEN=<jwt>  if you have a pre-obtained token."

# ==============================================================================
# Stack targets
# ==============================================================================

up:
	$(DC) up -d --build

up-webapp:
	$(DC) up -d --build $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

down:
	$(DC) down

down-webapp:
	$(DC) stop $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

logs:
	$(DC) logs -f

logs-webapp:
	$(DC) logs -f $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

status:
	$(DC) ps

clean:
	$(DC) down -v --remove-orphans

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark: setup and auth check
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-setup:
	@echo "Ensuring benchmarking configuration exists..."
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	curl -s -X GET "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)/api/v1/configurations/$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		-H "Authorization: Bearer $$TOKEN" | grep -q "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" || \
		(echo "  Config $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID) not found. It should be seeded automatically on config-service startup." && \
		 echo "  If you just started the services, wait a few seconds and try again." && \
		 exit 1)
	@echo "  Benchmarking configuration OK"

benchmark-auth-check:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	echo "  Token length: $${#TOKEN}"; \
	echo "  benchmark-auth-check: OK"

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-study — main entry point (runs inside Docker, idempotent)
#
# Starts the benchmark container, then launches run_all_benchmarks.sh inside
# it with the chosen SUITE.  Each dataset gets a stable RUN_NAME so re-running
# the same command resumes from where it left off.
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-study: benchmark-setup
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	echo "Running suite='$(SUITE)' inside container..."; \
	INP="$(DATASET)"; [ -z "$$INP" ] && INP="$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)"; \
	if [ -n "$$INP" ]; then \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/dev/null" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
				--input "$$INP" \
				--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
				--output-dir "/app/src/evaluation/results" \
				$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
				--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
				--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
				--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
				--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
				--token "$$TOKEN" \
				--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
				--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
				--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
				$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
				$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_EVALUATE),--only-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_JUDGE),--only-judge,) \
				$(if $(SKIP_EVALUATE),--skip-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
				$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",); \
	else \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/datasets" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			bash /app/src/evaluation/run_all_benchmarks.sh; \
	fi

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-comparative — single dataset, runs on host (fast iteration)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-comparative: benchmark-setup
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
		$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-rejudge — add new judges to an existing run (no re-creation)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously started run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-rejudge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--rejudge \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-reaggregate — recompute scores/kappa from stored judge data (no LLM)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously evaluated run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-reaggregate:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--reaggregate \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-judge — skip creation, run LLM-as-judge on existing snapshots
# Combines --only-evaluate --rejudge --reaggregate in one step.
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously created run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-judge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--only-judge \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-report — generate markdown/CSV report from an existing run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-report:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_DIR)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_DIR (e.g., evaluation/results/benchmark_ex_01_audit)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparison_reporter "$(RUN_DIR)"

# ==============================================================================
# Legacy / convenience targets (kept for backwards compatibility)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-create:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode create \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-evaluate:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode evaluate \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN"

benchmark-run-all:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode both \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-baselines:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	RUN_TS=$$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); \
	CONSOLE_LOG="$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)/baselines_console_$${RUN_TS}.log"; \
	mkdir -p "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)"; \
	echo "Running baselines on $(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)  (log: $$CONSOLE_LOG)"; \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
			--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
			--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
			--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			--token "$$TOKEN" \
			--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
			--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
	2>&1 | tee "$$CONSOLE_LOG"

benchmark-run-server:
	@echo "Launching server-side benchmark auto-runner (profile: autorun)..."
	@echo "All services must already be up (make up)."
	@echo "Results -> evaluation/results/ (mounted volume)"
	docker compose --profile autorun up --build benchmark-runner

benchmark-run-unified:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \

AUTH_POSTGRES_DB=auth_db

BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=adminadmin

# ── LLM Provider ─────────────────────────────────────────────

# ─── Feature Flags ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LAMINAR_ENABLED=false
RAG_ENABLED=false
OBSERVABILITY_BACKEND=console

displayContent.text:
import asyncio as _asyncio
        self.metrics["llm_calls"] += 1
        
        # Log the prompt to console for real-time inspection
        print(f"\n      [LLM CALL {self.metrics['llm_calls']}] Sending prompt ({len(messages)} messages)...", flush=True)
        for msg in messages:
            content_preview = (msg['content'][:200] + "...") if len(msg['content']) > 200 else msg['content']
            print(f"      --- {msg['role'].upper()} ---\n      {content_preview}", flush=True)

        for _attempt in range(5):
            try:
                safe_messages = trim_messages_to_token_budget(messages)
                response = await litellm.acompletion(
                    model=self.model_name,
                    messages=safe_messages,
                    temperature=0,
                    **get_litellm_kwargs(self.model_name),
                )
                break
            except Exception as _exc:
                if _attempt < 4 and "too many concurrent" in str(_exc).lower():
                    wait = 30 * (2 ** _attempt)
                    print(f"      [direct_llm] Ollama busy, retrying in {wait}s…", flush=True)
                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
                else:
                    raise

199hj|            for fname, finfo in props.items():

202nd|
203ml|                ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
204gs|                is_ref = finfo.get("is_reference", False)

229wn|        if topology_yaml:

displayContent.text:
"""
Baseline implementations for comparative evaluation.

Four generation approaches:
1. Direct LLM Generation — single-pass JSON output
2. Basic ReAct Agent — iterative tool use without orchestration
3. Code-Generation Agent — LLM writes Python, we exec() it, then persist
4. Full System — metamodel-driven agentic architecture (via autonomous_benchmark)
"""

import os
from typing import Any, Dict, List


def resolve_baseline_model() -> str:
    """
    Resolve the LiteLLM-formatted model name for baseline LLM calls.

    Priority order:
    1. BASELINE_MODEL  — explicit override for baselines specifically
    2. AGENT_MODEL     — already in litellm format (e.g. "ollama/qwen3-coder:cloud")
    3. AGENT_LARGE_MODEL + AGENT_PROVIDER  — constructed from orchestrator env vars

    The provider prefix (e.g. "ollama/") is added if missing.
    """
    # Explicit baseline override
    explicit = os.getenv("BASELINE_MODEL", "").strip()
    if explicit:
        return explicit

    # Already litellm-formatted
    agent_model = os.getenv("AGENT_MODEL", "").strip()
    if agent_model:
        return agent_model

    # Construct from AGENT_LARGE_MODEL + AGENT_PROVIDER
    large_model = os.getenv("AGENT_LARGE_MODEL", "").strip()
    provider = os.getenv("AGENT_PROVIDER", "ollama").strip().lower()
    if large_model:
        # Add provider prefix if not already present
        known_providers = ("ollama/", "openai/", "anthropic/", "openrouter/", "azure/", "groq/", "cohere/")
        if not any(large_model.startswith(p) for p in known_providers):
            return f"{provider}/{large_model}"
        return large_model

    # Final fallback
    return "ollama/qwen3-coder-next:cloud"


def _estimate_text_tokens(text: str) -> int:
    # Conservative heuristic when tokenizer metadata is unavailable.
    return max(1, len(text) // 4)


def _message_text(msg: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    content = msg.get("content", "")
    if isinstance(content, str):
        return content
    if isinstance(content, list):
        parts: List[str] = []
        for part in content:
            if isinstance(part, dict):
                parts.append(str(part.get("text", part)))
            else:
                parts.append(str(part))
        return " ".join(parts)
    return str(content)


def trim_messages_to_token_budget(
    messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    max_tokens: int = int(os.getenv("BASELINE_PROMPT_MAX_TOKENS", "28672")),
) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    if not messages:
        return messages

    trimmed = list(messages)
    while len(trimmed) > 1:
        estimated = sum(_estimate_text_tokens(_message_text(m)) for m in trimmed)
        if estimated <= max_tokens:
            break
        drop_idx = 1 if trimmed[0].get("role") == "system" and len(trimmed) > 1 else 0
        trimmed.pop(drop_idx)
    return trimmed


def get_litellm_kwargs(model_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    timeout_seconds = int(os.getenv("LITELLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "600"))
    kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {"timeout": timeout_seconds}

    provider = os.getenv("AGENT_PROVIDER", "ollama").strip().lower()
    if model_name.startswith("ollama/") or provider == "ollama":
        num_ctx = int(os.getenv("OLLAMA_NUM_CTX", "32768"))
        opts: Dict[str, Any] = {"num_ctx": num_ctx}

        # Qwen3 models run extended thinking by default, which causes the model
        # to reason about output-format instructions instead of obeying them.
        # Disable thinking for all baseline calls so JSON-only prompts are
        # respected literally.
        if "qwen3" in model_name.lower():
            opts["think"] = False

        kwargs["num_ctx"] = num_ctx
        kwargs["extra_body"] = {"options": opts}

    return kwargs


def classify_error(error: str) -> str:
    """Classify an error string into a structured category for analysis."""
    e = error.lower()
    # Format errors
    if "json" in e and ("decode" in e or "parse" in e or "invalid" in e):
        return "format:json_parse"
    if "no valid json" in e or "no valid artifacts" in e:
        return "format:no_json_output"
    if "not json serializable" in e or "is not json serializable" in e:
        return "format:serialization"
    if "syntaxerror" in e or "syntax error" in e:
        return "exec:syntax_error"
    # Schema / metamodel errors
    if "invalid class names" in e or "unknown model keys" in e:
        return "schema:invalid_class_names"
    if "validation error" in e and "pydantic" in e:
        return "schema:pydantic_validation"
    if "missing required" in e or "missing_argument" in e:
        return "schema:missing_required_field"
    if "unexpected keyword" in e:
        return "schema:unexpected_field"
    if "keyerror" in e and "_id" in error:
        return "schema:private_field_access"
    if "keyerror" in e:
        return "schema:key_error"
    if "typeerror" in e:
        return "exec:type_error"
    if "nameerror" in e or "attributeerror" in e:
        return "exec:name_or_attr_error"
    # Persistence / MCP errors
    if "create_artifact" in e or "batch_set_fields" in e or "set_reference" in e:
        return "persistence:mcp_tool_error"
    if "traceback" in e and "exec" in e:
        return "exec:python_runtime"
    # API / auth errors
    if "401" in error:
        return "api:unauthorized"
    if "403" in error:
        return "api:forbidden"
    if "timeout" in e:
        return "api:timeout"
    if "connection" in e or "connect" in e:
        return "api:connection"
    return "unknown"


def build_compact_schema_text(
    models: Dict[str, Any],
    topology_yaml: str = "",
) -> str:
    """
    Build a compact, LLM-friendly schema description from metamodel classes.

    This mirrors the information the full-system worker receives: field names,
    types, required markers, reference targets, and enum values — but in a
    single text block instead of per-entity tool calls.

    This ensures baselines get the SAME metamodel knowledge as the full system,
    making comparisons fair.
    """
    try:
        from autonomous.metamodel_analyzer import MetamodelAnalyzer
        analyzer = MetamodelAnalyzer(models)
        analyzer.analyze()

        # Get creation phases for ordering guidance
        phases = analyzer._graph.get_creation_phases()
        phase_map: Dict[str, int] = {}
        for phase in phases:
            for entity_name in phase.entities:
                phase_map[entity_name] = phase.phase_index

        # Sort classes by creation phase (dependencies first)
        sorted_classes = sorted(models.keys(), key=lambda c: phase_map.get(c, 99))

        lines: List[str] = []
        lines.append("METAMODEL CLASSES (ordered by creation phase — create earlier phases first):")
        lines.append("")

        for class_name in sorted_classes:
            phase = phase_map.get(class_name, 0)
            llm_schema = analyzer.to_llm_schema(class_name, phase_map, set())
            desc = llm_schema.get("description", "")
            required = set(llm_schema.get("required", []))
            props = llm_schema.get("properties", {})

            lines.append(f"### {class_name} (phase {phase})")
            if desc:
                lines.append(f"  Description: {desc}")

            for fname, finfo in props.items():
                req = "*" if fname in required else " "
                ftype = finfo.get("type", "?")

                ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
                is_ref = finfo.get("is_reference", False)
                cardinality = finfo.get("cardinality", "")
                enum_vals = finfo.get("enum_values", [])

                extra_parts: List[str] = []
                if is_ref:
                    arrow = f"-> {ref_target}" if ref_target else "-> ?"
                    card = f" [{cardinality}]" if cardinality else ""
                    extra_parts.append(f"REFERENCE{card} {arrow}")
                if enum_vals:
                    vals = ", ".join(str(v) for v in enum_vals[:10])
                    extra_parts.append(f"enum: [{vals}]")

                extra = f"  ({', '.join(extra_parts)})" if extra_parts else ""
                lines.append(f"  {req} {fname}: {ftype}{extra}")

            lines.append("")

        # Add creation order summary
        lines.append("CREATION ORDER (by dependency phase):")
        for phase_obj in phases:
            entities = ", ".join(sorted(phase_obj.entities))
            lines.append(f"  Phase {phase_obj.phase_index}: {entities}")
        lines.append("")

        if topology_yaml:
            lines.append("DEPENDENCY GRAPH:")
            lines.append(topology_yaml)

        return "\n".join(lines)

    except Exception as exc:
        # Fallback: use raw JSON schemas if analyzer fails
        import json as _json
        parts = [f"### {n}\n{_json.dumps(m.model_json_schema(), indent=2)}" for n, m in models.items()]
        schema = "\n\n".join(parts)
        if topology_yaml:
            schema += f"\n\nDEPENDENCY GRAPH:\n{topology_yaml}"
        return schema


from evaluation.baselines.baseline_direct_llm import BaselineDirectLLM
from evaluation.baselines.baseline_react_agent import BaselineReActAgent
from evaluation.baselines.baseline_code_gen import BaselineCodeGen

__all__ = [
    "BaselineDirectLLM",
    "BaselineReActAgent",
    "BaselineCodeGen",
    "resolve_baseline_model",
    "get_litellm_kwargs",
    "trim_messages_to_token_budget",
    "build_compact_schema_text",
]

"""
Baseline 1: Direct LLM Generation.
Single-pass: give the LLM the metamodel schemas, ask for a JSON object mapping
class names to lists of instances, then persist via MCP.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import re
import time
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Tuple, Type, Optional

import litellm

import sys
from pathlib import Path
CURRENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent.parent
if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))
ORCHESTRATOR_DIR = SRC_DIR / "langgraph-orchestrator"
if str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR))

from evaluation.baselines import (
    resolve_baseline_model,
    classify_error,
    get_litellm_kwargs,
    trim_messages_to_token_budget,
    build_compact_schema_text,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class BaselineDirectLLM:
    def __init__(
        self,
        models: Dict[str, Type[Any]],
        mcp_server_url: str = "http://localhost:12013/mcp",
        model_name: Optional[str] = None,
        topology_yaml: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.models = models
        self.mcp_server_url = mcp_server_url
        self.model_name = model_name or resolve_baseline_model()
        self.topology_yaml = topology_yaml
        self.metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "total_tokens": 0, "prompt_tokens": 0, "completion_tokens": 0,
            "llm_calls": 0, "retries": 0, "validation_errors": 0,
            "artifacts_created": 0, "duration": 0,
        }
        self.logs: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def _call_llm(self, messages: List[Dict[str, str]]) -> Tuple[str, Any]:
        import asyncio as _asyncio
        self.metrics["llm_calls"] += 1
        
        # Log the prompt to console for real-time inspection
        print(f"\n      [LLM CALL {self.metrics['llm_calls']}] Sending prompt ({len(messages)} messages)...", flush=True)
        for msg in messages:
            content_preview = (msg['content'][:200] + "...") if len(msg['content']) > 200 else msg['content']
            print(f"      --- {msg['role'].upper()} ---\n      {content_preview}", flush=True)

        for _attempt in range(5):
            try:
                safe_messages = trim_messages_to_token_budget(messages)
                response = await litellm.acompletion(
                    model=self.model_name,
                    messages=safe_messages,
                    temperature=0,
                    **get_litellm_kwargs(self.model_name),
                )
                break
            except Exception as _exc:
                if _attempt < 4 and "too many concurrent" in str(_exc).lower():
                    wait = 30 * (2 ** _attempt)
                    print(f"      [direct_llm] Ollama busy, retrying in {wait}s…", flush=True)
                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
                else:
                    raise
        
        usage = response.get("usage", {})
        self.metrics["prompt_tokens"] += usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
        self.metrics["completion_tokens"] += usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
        self.metrics["total_tokens"] += usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
        
        content = response.choices[0].message.content
        print(f"      --- RESPONSE ---\n      {content[:500]}...", flush=True)
        return content, response

    def _get_schema_text(self) -> str:
        return build_compact_schema_text(self.models, self.topology_yaml or "")

    def _extract_json(self, text: str) -> Any:
        for pattern in [r"```(?:json)?\s*\n?([\s\S]*?)```", None]:
            if pattern:
                m = re.search(pattern, text)
                if m:
                    try:
                        return json.loads(m.group(1))
                    except Exception:
                        pass
        for s, e in [("{", "}"), ("[", "]")]:
            start = text.find(s)
            if start != -1:
                try:
                    return json.loads(text[start:text.rfind(e) + 1])
                except Exception:
                    pass
        return None

    def _filter_valid_model_keys(self, parsed: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Keep only keys that match known metamodel class names."""
        if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
            return {}
        valid = {k: v for k, v in parsed.items() if k in self.models}
        invalid = [k for k in parsed if k not in self.models]
        if invalid:
            logger.warning("direct_llm: ignoring unknown model keys: %s", invalid)
        return valid

    async def generate(
        self, description: str, project_id: str = "", log_callback: Optional[callable] = None
    ) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
        start = time.perf_counter()
        valid_classes = ", ".join(self.models.keys())
        schema_text = self._get_schema_text()

        # Keep the system message as a brief role-assignment only.
        # Qwen3 (and similar reasoning models) treat large system-message content
        # as a document to analyse rather than as instructions to follow.
        # Putting the schema + task in the user turn produces reliable JSON output.
        system_prompt = (
            "You are a cyber-range infrastructure designer. "
            "Your only job is to output structured JSON data. "
            "Never explain, summarise, or analyse — respond with a raw JSON object only."
        )
        user_prompt = (
            f"Generate a complete cyber-range scenario for the following exercise.\n\n"
            f"EXERCISE:\n{description}\n\n"
            f"{schema_text}\n\n"
            f"OUTPUT FORMAT:\n"
            f"Return ONLY a single JSON object. No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation.\n"
            f"Top-level keys MUST be metamodel class names drawn exclusively from this list:\n"
            f"  [{valid_classes}]\n"
            f"Each value is a list of fully-populated instance objects.\n\n"
            f"FIELD RULES:\n"
            f"- Fields marked with * are REQUIRED — you must provide a value.\n"
            f"- REFERENCE fields point to other artifacts. Use a unique string ID (e.g. UUID)\n"
            f"  that matches the 'id' or 'uuid' field of the referenced artifact instance.\n"
            f"- Enum fields must use one of the listed values.\n"
            f"- Populate ALL fields for each instance, not just the class name.\n"
            f"  Include scalar fields (name, description, ip, cidr, etc.) AND reference fields.\n\n"
            f"IMPORTANT:\n"
            f"- Top-level keys must be ONLY from [{valid_classes}].\n"
            f"- Do NOT invent wrapper keys like 'cyber_range', 'infrastructure', 'scenario', etc.\n"
            f"- Create instances following the creation order (phase 0 first, then phase 1, etc.).\n"
            f"- Each instance MUST have an 'id' or 'uuid' field so other instances can reference it.\n\n"
            f"JSON output:"
        )
        messages = [
            {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
            {"role": "user", "content": user_prompt},
        ]

        self.logs.append({
            "event": "start",
            "model": self.model_name,
            "mcp_url": self.mcp_server_url,
            "project_id": project_id,
            "valid_classes": list(self.models.keys()),
            "description": description,
        })
        if log_callback:
            log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)

        current_artifacts: Dict[str, Any] = {}

        for attempt in range(4):
            raw, _ = await self._call_llm(messages)
            parsed = self._extract_json(raw)

            self.logs.append({
                "event": "llm_call",
                "attempt": attempt,
                "messages": [{"role": m["role"], "content": m["content"]} for m in messages],
                "response": raw,
                "parsed_ok": parsed is not None,
                "token_totals": {
                    "prompt": self.metrics["prompt_tokens"],
                    "completion": self.metrics["completion_tokens"],
                    "total": self.metrics["total_tokens"],
                },
            })
            if log_callback:
                log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
            print(f"      [direct_llm] attempt {attempt}: parsed={parsed is not None}, "
                  f"tokens={self.metrics['total_tokens']}", flush=True)

            if parsed is not None:
                valid = self._filter_valid_model_keys(parsed)
                if valid:
                    current_artifacts = valid
                    break
                # Parsed but no valid class names — retry with hint
                invalid_keys = list(parsed.keys())
                err_msg = f"Invalid class names used: {invalid_keys}"
                self.logs[-1]["error"] = err_msg
                self.logs[-1]["error_class"] = classify_error(err_msg)
                messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": raw})
                messages.append({
                    "role": "user",
                    "content": (
                        f"WRONG: you used invalid class names: {invalid_keys}.\n"
                        f"You MUST use ONLY these exact class names as top-level keys:\n"
                        f"  [{valid_classes}]\n"
                        f"Output ONLY the corrected JSON object. No explanation."
                    ),
                })
                self.metrics["retries"] += 1
                continue

            err_msg = "No valid JSON found in LLM response"
            self.logs[-1]["error"] = err_msg
            self.logs[-1]["error_class"] = classify_error(err_msg)
            messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": raw})
            messages.append({
                "role": "user",
                "content": (
                    f"Your response was not valid JSON.\n"
                    f"Output ONLY a raw JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences.\n"
                    f"Top-level keys must be class names from: [{valid_classes}].\n"
                    f"JSON output:"
                ),
            })
            self.metrics["retries"] += 1

        if current_artifacts:
            counts = {k: len(v) if isinstance(v, list) else 1 for k, v in current_artifacts.items()}
            self.metrics["artifacts_created"] = sum(counts.values())
            self.logs.append({
                "event": "artifacts_produced",
                "artifact_counts_by_model": counts,
                "total_artifacts": sum(counts.values()),
            })
            print(f"      [direct_llm] produced {sum(counts.values())} artifacts: {counts}", flush=True)
        else:
            self.logs.append({"event": "artifacts_produced", "skipped": True, "reason": "no valid artifacts produced"})
            print("      [direct_llm] no valid artifacts produced", flush=True)

        self.metrics["duration"] = time.perf_counter() - start
        self.logs.append({"event": "done", "metrics": dict(self.metrics)})
        return current_artifacts, self.logs

1ah|"""
2ek|Baseline 2: ReAct Agent baseline.
3rd|
4id|Models the real WorkerAgent loop: JSON-only output format, system+user conversation
5it|turns, explicit tool documentation, finish()/fail() terminators.  The only
6eq|difference from a real worker is that this agent creates ALL artifacts for the
7ch|full exercise (no subsystem partitioning, no spawn_worker delegation).
8oe|"""
9th|

11th|

20vg|import litellm
21st|

25zl|SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent.parent
26qy|if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
27kd|    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))

29fc|from evaluation.baselines import (
30el|    resolve_baseline_model,
31kc|    classify_error,
32tk|    get_litellm_kwargs,
33ip|    trim_messages_to_token_budget,
34pz|    build_compact_schema_text,
35ca|)
36th|
37mv|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
38th|
39ky|MAX_STEPS = 1000
40kd|MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 15
41st|
42ug|# context window management
43jl|CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD = 18  # compress when history exceeds this many steps
44ls|CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT = 8          # always keep this many recent steps in full detail

46th|
47ai|class BaselineReActAgent:
48fs|    def __init__(
49ss|        self,
50ld|        models: Dict[str, Type[Any]],
51mm|        mcp_server_url: str = "http://localhost:12013/mcp",
52lu|        token: str = "",
53mj|        topology_yaml: Optional[str] = None,
54ci|    ):
55au|        self.models = models
56ad|        self.mcp_server_url = mcp_server_url
57ip|        self.token = token
58an|        self.topology_yaml = topology_yaml
59gv|        self.metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {
60uy|            "total_tokens": 0, "prompt_tokens": 0, "completion_tokens": 0,
61et|            "tool_calls": 0, "tool_failures": 0, "llm_calls": 0, "iterations": 0, "duration": 0,
62nd|        }
63vd|        self.compressed_history_summary = ""
64rz|        self.logs: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
65th|
66la|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
67ya|    # LLM CALL
68zf|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
69th|
70nn|    async def _call_llm(self, messages: List[Dict[str, str]]) -> str:
71ku|        import asyncio as _asyncio
72aw|        self.metrics["llm_calls"] += 1
73az|        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()
74th|
75tw|        # Log prompt preview to console
76ab|        print(f"\n      [RE-ACT STEP {self.metrics['llm_calls']}] Sending prompt to {model_name}...", flush=True)
77fc|        # Show only recent messages to avoid cluttering console
78ba|        recent = messages[-2:] if len(messages) >= 2 else messages
79xm|        for msg in recent:
80uy|            preview = (msg['content'][:300] + "...") if len(msg['content']) > 300 else msg['content']
81vd|            print(f"      --- {msg['role'].upper()} ---\n      {preview}", flush=True)
82nd|
83kx|        for _attempt in range(5):
84xy|            try:
85hp|                safe_messages = trim_messages_to_token_budget(messages)
86un|                response = await litellm.acompletion(
87fu|                    model=model_name,
88ch|                    messages=safe_messages,
89em|                    temperature=0,
90zr|                    **get_litellm_kwargs(model_name),
91lu|                )
92jo|                usage = response.get("usage", {})
93bg|                self.metrics["prompt_tokens"] += usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
94oh|                self.metrics["completion_tokens"] += usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
95yv|                self.metrics["total_tokens"] += usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
96th|                
97os|                content = response.choices[0].message.content
98ep|                print(f"      --- ASSISTANT RESPONSE ---\n      {content[:400]}...", flush=True)
99la|                return content
100jh|            except Exception as _exc:
101do|                if _attempt < 4 and "too many concurrent" in str(_exc).lower():
102bb|                    wait = 30 * (2 ** _attempt)
103ul|                    print(f"      [react_agent] Ollama busy, retrying in {wait}s…", flush=True)
104md|                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
105oh|                else:
106er|                    raise
107wu|        raise RuntimeError("LLM call failed after 5 attempts")

109ho|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
