113ea|    def _build_system_prompt(self, description: str, project_id: str) -> str:
114pi|        schema_text = build_compact_schema_text(self.models, self.topology_yaml or "")
115th|
116cx|        return f"""You are a Worker Agent executing a cyber-exercise creation task.
117ej|You must create ALL artifacts required to fully implement the exercise described below.
118th|
119ac|YOUR ASSIGNMENT:
120vx|{description}
121st|
122jj|PROJECT ID (injected automatically — do NOT pass it as a tool argument):
123xo|{project_id}
124th|
125sw|{schema_text}
126th|
127vq|FIELD POPULATION RULES:
128ep|- Fields marked with * are REQUIRED — you must provide a value.
129jd|- REFERENCE fields (marked with "-> TargetClass") point to other artifacts.
130rs|  Use set_reference or append_to_collection for these — NEVER put them in batch_set_fields.
131iv|- Enum fields must use one of the listed values.
132oy|- Populate ALL scalar fields (name, description, ip, cidr, etc.) via batch_set_fields.
133nf|- Create artifacts following the phase order (phase 0 first, then phase 1, etc.).
134th|
135nm|AVAILABLE TOOLS:
136th|
137ia|  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
138zs|  🔧 ARTIFACT MANAGEMENT TOOLS
139pt|  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
140th|
141ze|  query_artifacts(model_name)
142yg|    → List existing artifacts of a type.
143bp|    → Returns: [{{"artifact_id": "...", "data": {{...}}}}]
144th|
145cr|  get_artifact(artifact_id)
146xl|    → Inspect an existing artifact.
147he|    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact": {{...}}}}
148th|
149fz|  get_model_schema(model_name)
150db|    → Get detailed field information for a model (use to discover field names).
151ia|    → Returns: {{"success": true, "schema": {{...}}}}
152nd|
153jl|  create_artifact(model_name, common_name?)
154fr|    → Create a new empty artifact shell.
155qr|    → Parameters: model_name (string), common_name (optional short label)
156vj|    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact_id": "<UUID>"}}
157ui|    → ⚠️  The returned artifact_id UUID is what you use in ALL subsequent calls.
158th|
159tg|  batch_set_fields(artifact_id, fields_json)
160tr|    → Set SCALAR (primitive) fields: strings, numbers, booleans, enums.
161xi|    → Parameters:
162hc|        • artifact_id: UUID from create_artifact
163ir|        • fields_json: JSON string with field→value pairs (scalars only)
164os|    → ❌ NEVER put reference fields here. Reference fields (pointing to other
165vi|         artifacts) MUST use set_reference or append_to_collection.
166up|    → Returns: {{"success": true}} or {{"success": false, "error": "..."}}
167th|
168ei|  set_reference(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
169bi|    → Link a single-valued reference field to another artifact.
170xi|    → Parameters:
171zd|        • artifact_id: UUID of the artifact being modified
172ct|        • field_name: the reference field name (from get_model_schema)
173ul|        • reference_value: UUID of the artifact to reference
174zd|    → Returns: {{"success": true}}
175th|
176ap|  append_to_collection(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
177gj|    → Append to a list/collection reference field.
178zi|    → Parameters same as set_reference.
179zd|    → Returns: {{"success": true}}
180th|
181bh|  validate_artifact(artifact_id)
182yj|    → Validate against the metamodel schema.
183uc|    → Returns: {{"valid": true}} or {{"valid": false, "errors": [...]}}
184th|
185sm|  commit_artifact(artifact_id)
186pr|    → Finalize the artifact. Call ONLY after validate returns valid=true.
187zd|    → Returns: {{"success": true}}
188th|
189bn|  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
190gw|  ✅ COMPLETION SIGNALS
191sa|  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
192nd|
193gz|  finish()
194bx|    → Signal that ALL artifacts are created, validated, and committed.
195nf|    → Call this as your LAST action when the full exercise is done.
196th|
197qb|  fail(reason)
198ud|    → Signal that you cannot complete the task. Use only if truly stuck.
199th|
200ji|RULES:
201om|  1. Use get_model_schema(model_name) to discover field names and types before
202ko|     creating an artifact if you are unsure of its fields.
203rz|  2. Call create_artifact once per artifact to get its UUID.
204ut|  3. Call batch_set_fields with ALL scalar fields in a single call per artifact.
205ub|  4. Use set_reference / append_to_collection for any field that points to another
206xo|     artifact — NEVER pass artifact data as a dict inside batch_set_fields.
207qi|  5. Call validate_artifact before commit_artifact.
208hs|  6. Fix validation errors before committing.
209mw|  7. Call commit_artifact only after validation passes.
210ai|  8. When all artifacts are committed, call finish().
211jf|  9. NEVER repeat a tool call with the exact same arguments — reuse results from history.
212em|  10. Do NOT pass project_id as a tool argument — it is injected automatically.
213lc|  11. Build artifacts bottom-up: create dependencies (Networks, Hardware) before
214fb|      the artifacts that reference them (Nodes, VMs).
215th|
216hm|OUTPUT FORMAT — respond with ONLY valid JSON, no prose, no markdown:
217ax|{{"tool": "tool_name_here", "args": {{"arg1": "value1"}}}}
218th|
219wj|Examples:
220kh|  {{"tool": "get_model_schema", "args": {{"model_name": "Node"}}}}
221ca|  {{"tool": "create_artifact", "args": {{"model_name": "Network", "common_name": "hq_lan"}}}}
222gb|  {{"tool": "batch_set_fields", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "fields_json": "{{\\"name\\": \\"HQ LAN\\", \\"cidr\\": \\"10.0.0.0/24\\"}}"}}}}
223dy|  {{"tool": "set_reference", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "field_name": "topology", "reference_value": "<topology_uuid>"}}}}
224za|  {{"tool": "validate_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
225bo|  {{"tool": "commit_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
226fp|  {{"tool": "finish", "args": {{}}}}
227ue|"""
228th|
229yw|    def _maybe_compress_history(self, tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
230tg|        """Rolling compression of tool history to stay within context limits."""
231hx|        if len(tool_history) <= CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD:
232hx|            return tool_history
233rd|
234we|        to_compress = tool_history[:-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT]
235bn|        remaining = tool_history[-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT:]
236th|
237ec|        lines: List[str] = []
238co|        create_ids: List[str] = []
239ys|        fields_set: List[str] = []
240pw|        validations_passed = 0
241kv|        failures = 0
242nd|
243hp|        for entry in to_compress:
244ur|            tool_name = entry.get("tool")
245ud|            res = entry.get("result", {})
246xh|            success = entry.get("success", True)
247th|            
248rg|            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and success:
249pr|                aid = res.get("artifact_id", "?")
250jy|                create_ids.append(aid)
251ry|            elif tool_name == "batch_set_fields" and success:
252av|                try:
253mc|                    fkeys = list(json.loads(entry.get("args", {}).get("fields_json", "{}")).keys())
254an|                    fields_set.extend(fkeys)
255mu|                except Exception:
256jj|                    pass
257sr|            elif tool_name == "validate_artifact":
258cu|                if res.get("valid"):
259qr|                    validations_passed += 1
260dp|            if not success:
261cl|                failures += 1
262nd|
263kk|        if create_ids:
264mq|            lines.append(f"Created artifact(s): {', '.join(create_ids)}")
265cy|        if fields_set:
266pu|            unique_fields = list(dict.fromkeys(fields_set))
267cc|            lines.append(f"Fields set: {', '.join(unique_fields[:20])}")
268zk|        if validations_passed:
269iv|            lines.append(f"Validation passed {validations_passed} time(s)")
270vn|        if failures:
271gf|            lines.append(f"Tool failures encountered: {failures}")
272nd|
273br|        summary_chunk = f"[Steps 1-{len(to_compress)} compressed] " + "; ".join(lines) + "."
274en|        if self.compressed_history_summary:
275tl|            self.compressed_history_summary += "\n" + summary_chunk
276ke|        else:
277tf|            self.compressed_history_summary = summary_chunk
278th|            
279an|        return remaining
280th|
281tz|    def _build_messages(
282ss|        self,
283fw|        system_prompt: str,
284ru|        tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]],
285vw|        consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]],
286pe|    ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
287dd|        messages: List[Dict[str, str]] = [{"role": "system", "content": system_prompt}]
288th|
289en|        if self.compressed_history_summary:
290sf|            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"PROGRESS SUMMARY (earlier steps):\n{self.compressed_history_summary}"})
291st|
292nx|        for entry in tool_history:
293tw|            messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": json.dumps({"tool": entry["tool"], "args": entry["args"]})})
294nw|            obs = json.dumps(entry["result"], default=str)
295ct|            if len(obs) > 4000:
296ry|                obs = obs[:4000] + "… [truncated]"
297rc|            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"OBSERVATION: {obs}"})
298th|
299lw|        if len(consecutive_failures) >= 2:
300da|            error_lines = "\n".join(
301wa|                f"  • {e['tool']}: {e['result'].get('error', 'failed')}"
302th|                for e in consecutive_failures
303ds|            )
304ii|            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
305ff|                f"⚠️  REPEATED TOOL FAILURES ({len(consecutive_failures)} in a row):\n"
306nm|                f"{error_lines}\n\n"
307wp|                "You must change your approach. Before your next action:\n"
308yr|                "1. State the ROOT CAUSE of these failures\n"
309cd|                "2. Describe your CORRECTED PLAN\n"
310vw|                "3. Then provide the corrected JSON action"
311qy|            )})
312yw|        elif len(consecutive_failures) == 1:
313ij|            e = consecutive_failures[0]
314ii|            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
315ej|                f"The previous {e['tool']} call failed: {e['result'].get('error', 'unknown error')}\n"
316vt|                "Briefly reason about what went wrong, then provide a corrected JSON action."
317gi|            )})
318th|
319cg|        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": "What is your next action? Respond with JSON only."})
320rd|        return messages
321st|
322gg|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
323al|    # JSON PARSER (same balanced-brace approach as WorkerAgent)
324lj|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
325th|
326nk|    def _parse_action(self, content: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
327ib|        content = content.strip()
328gf|        try:
329qb|            return json.loads(content)
330in|        except json.JSONDecodeError:
331sl|            pass
332hd|        code_block = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```", content)
333tx|        if code_block:
334xy|            try:
335ex|                return json.loads(code_block.group(1))
336mb|            except json.JSONDecodeError:
337jy|                pass
338st|        for start in range(len(content)):
339ps|            if content[start] != "{":
340yt|                continue
341wr|            depth, in_string, escape_next = 0, False, False
342nm|            for end in range(start, len(content)):
343on|                ch = content[end]
344jg|                if escape_next:
345wt|                    escape_next = False
346ll|                    continue
347ke|                if ch == "\\" and in_string:
348mm|                    escape_next = True
349ll|                    continue
350qm|                if ch == '"':
351ar|                    in_string = not in_string
352ll|                    continue
353tc|                if in_string:
354ll|                    continue
355nd|                if ch == "{":
356gy|                    depth += 1
357ol|                elif ch == "}":
358cp|                    depth -= 1
359ja|                    if depth == 0:
360nn|                        try:
361gu|                            return json.loads(content[start:end + 1])
362ls|                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
363yd|                            break
364ki|        return None
365th|
366jl|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
367ox|    # MAIN LOOP
368gh|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
369th|
370tk|    async def generate(
371st|        self, description: str, project_id: str = "", log_callback: Optional[callable] = None
372fy|    ) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
373mv|        start_time = time.perf_counter()
374az|        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()
375th|
376pr|        self.logs.append({
377xx|            "event": "start",
378yg|            "model": model_name,
379cd|            "mcp_url": self.mcp_server_url,
380ew|            "project_id": project_id,
381wy|            "description_preview": description[:200],
382ev|        })
383mm|        if log_callback:
384zw|            log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
385th|
386og|        transport = StreamableHttpTransport(url=self.mcp_server_url)
387jo|        async with Client(transport=transport) as mcp:
388us|            mcp_tools = await mcp.list_tools()
389xe|            tool_names = [t.name for t in mcp_tools]
390pu|            self.logs.append({"event": "tools_discovered", "tools": tool_names})
391hi|            if log_callback:
392ss|                log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
393nb|            print(f"      [react_agent] discovered {len(tool_names)} MCP tools", flush=True)
394th|
395co|            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(description, project_id)
396jy|            tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
397th|
398uf|            for step in range(MAX_STEPS):
399dc|                self.metrics["iterations"] += 1
400th|                
401nc|                # Compress history if it exceeds threshold
402io|                tool_history = self._maybe_compress_history(tool_history)
403rd|
404jr|                # Compute consecutive tail failures for error injection
405jg|                consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
406cz|                for entry in reversed(tool_history):

"""
Baseline 2: ReAct Agent baseline.

Models the real WorkerAgent loop: JSON-only output format, system+user conversation
turns, explicit tool documentation, finish()/fail() terminators.  The only
difference from a real worker is that this agent creates ALL artifacts for the
full exercise (no subsystem partitioning, no spawn_worker delegation).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import re
import time
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Type

from fastmcp import Client
from fastmcp.client.transports import StreamableHttpTransport
import litellm

import sys
from pathlib import Path
CURRENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent.parent
if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))

from evaluation.baselines import (
    resolve_baseline_model,
    classify_error,
    get_litellm_kwargs,
    trim_messages_to_token_budget,
    build_compact_schema_text,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

MAX_STEPS = 1000
MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 15

# context window management
CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD = 18  # compress when history exceeds this many steps
CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT = 8          # always keep this many recent steps in full detail


class BaselineReActAgent:
    def __init__(
        self,
        models: Dict[str, Type[Any]],
        mcp_server_url: str = "http://localhost:12013/mcp",
        token: str = "",
        topology_yaml: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.models = models
        self.mcp_server_url = mcp_server_url
        self.token = token
        self.topology_yaml = topology_yaml
        self.metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "total_tokens": 0, "prompt_tokens": 0, "completion_tokens": 0,
            "tool_calls": 0, "tool_failures": 0, "llm_calls": 0, "iterations": 0, "duration": 0,
        }
        self.compressed_history_summary = ""
        self.logs: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # LLM CALL
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def _call_llm(self, messages: List[Dict[str, str]]) -> str:
        import asyncio as _asyncio
        self.metrics["llm_calls"] += 1
        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()

        # Log prompt preview to console
        print(f"\n      [RE-ACT STEP {self.metrics['llm_calls']}] Sending prompt to {model_name}...", flush=True)
        # Show only recent messages to avoid cluttering console
        recent = messages[-2:] if len(messages) >= 2 else messages
        for msg in recent:
            preview = (msg['content'][:300] + "...") if len(msg['content']) > 300 else msg['content']
            print(f"      --- {msg['role'].upper()} ---\n      {preview}", flush=True)

        for _attempt in range(5):
            try:
                safe_messages = trim_messages_to_token_budget(messages)
                response = await litellm.acompletion(
                    model=model_name,
                    messages=safe_messages,
                    temperature=0,
                    **get_litellm_kwargs(model_name),
                )
                usage = response.get("usage", {})
                self.metrics["prompt_tokens"] += usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
                self.metrics["completion_tokens"] += usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
                self.metrics["total_tokens"] += usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
                
                content = response.choices[0].message.content
                print(f"      --- ASSISTANT RESPONSE ---\n      {content[:400]}...", flush=True)
                return content
            except Exception as _exc:
                if _attempt < 4 and "too many concurrent" in str(_exc).lower():
                    wait = 30 * (2 ** _attempt)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] Ollama busy, retrying in {wait}s…", flush=True)
                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
                else:
                    raise
        raise RuntimeError("LLM call failed after 5 attempts")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PROMPT CONSTRUCTION
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _build_system_prompt(self, description: str, project_id: str) -> str:
        schema_text = build_compact_schema_text(self.models, self.topology_yaml or "")

        return f"""You are a Worker Agent executing a cyber-exercise creation task.
You must create ALL artifacts required to fully implement the exercise described below.

YOUR ASSIGNMENT:
{description}

PROJECT ID (injected automatically — do NOT pass it as a tool argument):
{project_id}

{schema_text}

FIELD POPULATION RULES:
- Fields marked with * are REQUIRED — you must provide a value.
- REFERENCE fields (marked with "-> TargetClass") point to other artifacts.
  Use set_reference or append_to_collection for these — NEVER put them in batch_set_fields.
- Enum fields must use one of the listed values.
- Populate ALL scalar fields (name, description, ip, cidr, etc.) via batch_set_fields.
- Create artifacts following the phase order (phase 0 first, then phase 1, etc.).

AVAILABLE TOOLS:

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  🔧 ARTIFACT MANAGEMENT TOOLS
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  query_artifacts(model_name)
    → List existing artifacts of a type.
    → Returns: [{{"artifact_id": "...", "data": {{...}}}}]

  get_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Inspect an existing artifact.
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact": {{...}}}}

  get_model_schema(model_name)
    → Get detailed field information for a model (use to discover field names).
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "schema": {{...}}}}

  create_artifact(model_name, common_name?)
    → Create a new empty artifact shell.
    → Parameters: model_name (string), common_name (optional short label)
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact_id": "<UUID>"}}
    → ⚠️  The returned artifact_id UUID is what you use in ALL subsequent calls.

  batch_set_fields(artifact_id, fields_json)
    → Set SCALAR (primitive) fields: strings, numbers, booleans, enums.
    → Parameters:
        • artifact_id: UUID from create_artifact
        • fields_json: JSON string with field→value pairs (scalars only)
    → ❌ NEVER put reference fields here. Reference fields (pointing to other
         artifacts) MUST use set_reference or append_to_collection.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}} or {{"success": false, "error": "..."}}

  set_reference(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
    → Link a single-valued reference field to another artifact.
    → Parameters:
        • artifact_id: UUID of the artifact being modified
        • field_name: the reference field name (from get_model_schema)
        • reference_value: UUID of the artifact to reference
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  append_to_collection(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
    → Append to a list/collection reference field.
    → Parameters same as set_reference.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  validate_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Validate against the metamodel schema.
    → Returns: {{"valid": true}} or {{"valid": false, "errors": [...]}}

  commit_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Finalize the artifact. Call ONLY after validate returns valid=true.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  ✅ COMPLETION SIGNALS
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  finish()
    → Signal that ALL artifacts are created, validated, and committed.
    → Call this as your LAST action when the full exercise is done.

  fail(reason)
    → Signal that you cannot complete the task. Use only if truly stuck.

RULES:
  1. Use get_model_schema(model_name) to discover field names and types before
     creating an artifact if you are unsure of its fields.
  2. Call create_artifact once per artifact to get its UUID.
  3. Call batch_set_fields with ALL scalar fields in a single call per artifact.
  4. Use set_reference / append_to_collection for any field that points to another
     artifact — NEVER pass artifact data as a dict inside batch_set_fields.
  5. Call validate_artifact before commit_artifact.
  6. Fix validation errors before committing.
  7. Call commit_artifact only after validation passes.
  8. When all artifacts are committed, call finish().
  9. NEVER repeat a tool call with the exact same arguments — reuse results from history.
  10. Do NOT pass project_id as a tool argument — it is injected automatically.
  11. Build artifacts bottom-up: create dependencies (Networks, Hardware) before
      the artifacts that reference them (Nodes, VMs).

OUTPUT FORMAT — respond with ONLY valid JSON, no prose, no markdown:
{{"tool": "tool_name_here", "args": {{"arg1": "value1"}}}}

Examples:
  {{"tool": "get_model_schema", "args": {{"model_name": "Node"}}}}
  {{"tool": "create_artifact", "args": {{"model_name": "Network", "common_name": "hq_lan"}}}}
  {{"tool": "batch_set_fields", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "fields_json": "{{\\"name\\": \\"HQ LAN\\", \\"cidr\\": \\"10.0.0.0/24\\"}}"}}}}
  {{"tool": "set_reference", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "field_name": "topology", "reference_value": "<topology_uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "validate_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "commit_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "finish", "args": {{}}}}
"""

    def _maybe_compress_history(self, tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Rolling compression of tool history to stay within context limits."""
        if len(tool_history) <= CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD:
            return tool_history

        to_compress = tool_history[:-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT]
        remaining = tool_history[-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT:]

        lines: List[str] = []
        create_ids: List[str] = []
        fields_set: List[str] = []
        validations_passed = 0
        failures = 0

        for entry in to_compress:
            tool_name = entry.get("tool")
            res = entry.get("result", {})
            success = entry.get("success", True)
            
            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and success:
                aid = res.get("artifact_id", "?")
                create_ids.append(aid)
            elif tool_name == "batch_set_fields" and success:
                try:
                    fkeys = list(json.loads(entry.get("args", {}).get("fields_json", "{}")).keys())
                    fields_set.extend(fkeys)
                except Exception:
                    pass
            elif tool_name == "validate_artifact":
                if res.get("valid"):
                    validations_passed += 1
            if not success:
                failures += 1

        if create_ids:
            lines.append(f"Created artifact(s): {', '.join(create_ids)}")
        if fields_set:
            unique_fields = list(dict.fromkeys(fields_set))
            lines.append(f"Fields set: {', '.join(unique_fields[:20])}")
        if validations_passed:
            lines.append(f"Validation passed {validations_passed} time(s)")
        if failures:
            lines.append(f"Tool failures encountered: {failures}")

        summary_chunk = f"[Steps 1-{len(to_compress)} compressed] " + "; ".join(lines) + "."
        if self.compressed_history_summary:
            self.compressed_history_summary += "\n" + summary_chunk
        else:
            self.compressed_history_summary = summary_chunk
            
        return remaining

    def _build_messages(
        self,
        system_prompt: str,
        tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]],
        consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
        messages: List[Dict[str, str]] = [{"role": "system", "content": system_prompt}]

        if self.compressed_history_summary:
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"PROGRESS SUMMARY (earlier steps):\n{self.compressed_history_summary}"})

        for entry in tool_history:
            messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": json.dumps({"tool": entry["tool"], "args": entry["args"]})})
            obs = json.dumps(entry["result"], default=str)
            if len(obs) > 4000:
                obs = obs[:4000] + "… [truncated]"
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"OBSERVATION: {obs}"})

        if len(consecutive_failures) >= 2:
            error_lines = "\n".join(
                f"  • {e['tool']}: {e['result'].get('error', 'failed')}"
                for e in consecutive_failures
            )
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
                f"⚠️  REPEATED TOOL FAILURES ({len(consecutive_failures)} in a row):\n"
                f"{error_lines}\n\n"
                "You must change your approach. Before your next action:\n"
                "1. State the ROOT CAUSE of these failures\n"
                "2. Describe your CORRECTED PLAN\n"
                "3. Then provide the corrected JSON action"
            )})
        elif len(consecutive_failures) == 1:
            e = consecutive_failures[0]
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
                f"The previous {e['tool']} call failed: {e['result'].get('error', 'unknown error')}\n"
                "Briefly reason about what went wrong, then provide a corrected JSON action."
            )})

        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": "What is your next action? Respond with JSON only."})
        return messages

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # JSON PARSER (same balanced-brace approach as WorkerAgent)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _parse_action(self, content: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        content = content.strip()
        try:
            return json.loads(content)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            pass
        code_block = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```", content)
        if code_block:
            try:
                return json.loads(code_block.group(1))
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                pass
        for start in range(len(content)):
            if content[start] != "{":
                continue
            depth, in_string, escape_next = 0, False, False
            for end in range(start, len(content)):
                ch = content[end]
                if escape_next:
                    escape_next = False
                    continue
                if ch == "\\" and in_string:
                    escape_next = True
                    continue
                if ch == '"':
                    in_string = not in_string
                    continue
                if in_string:
                    continue
                if ch == "{":
                    depth += 1
                elif ch == "}":
                    depth -= 1
                    if depth == 0:
                        try:
                            return json.loads(content[start:end + 1])
                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
                            break
        return None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # MAIN LOOP
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def generate(
        self, description: str, project_id: str = "", log_callback: Optional[callable] = None
    ) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()

        self.logs.append({
            "event": "start",
            "model": model_name,
            "mcp_url": self.mcp_server_url,
            "project_id": project_id,
            "description_preview": description[:200],
        })
        if log_callback:
            log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)

        transport = StreamableHttpTransport(url=self.mcp_server_url)
        async with Client(transport=transport) as mcp:
            mcp_tools = await mcp.list_tools()
            tool_names = [t.name for t in mcp_tools]
            self.logs.append({"event": "tools_discovered", "tools": tool_names})
            if log_callback:
                log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
            print(f"      [react_agent] discovered {len(tool_names)} MCP tools", flush=True)

            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(description, project_id)
            tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

            for step in range(MAX_STEPS):
                self.metrics["iterations"] += 1
                
                # Compress history if it exceeds threshold
                tool_history = self._maybe_compress_history(tool_history)

                # Compute consecutive tail failures for error injection
                consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
                for entry in reversed(tool_history):
                    if not entry.get("success", True):
                        consecutive_failures.insert(0, entry)
                    else:
                        break

                if len(consecutive_failures) >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES:
                    self.logs.append({"event": "fatal_failures", "step": step, "count": len(consecutive_failures)})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: EXCEEDED {MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES} CONSECUTIVE FAILURES. Stopping.", flush=True)
                    break

                messages = self._build_messages(system_prompt, tool_history, consecutive_failures)
                raw = await self._call_llm(messages)
                action = self._parse_action(raw)

                if action is None:
                    self.logs.append({"event": "parse_failure", "step": step, "raw_preview": raw[:300]})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: could not parse JSON action", flush=True)
                    # Inject parse failure feedback and continue
                    tool_history.append({
                        "tool": "_parse_error",
                        "args": {},
                        "result": {"error": f"Your response was not valid JSON. Raw: {raw[:200]}"},
                        "success": False,
                    })
                    continue

                tool_name = action.get("tool", "")
                tool_args = action.get("args", {})

                # Termination signals
                if tool_name == "finish":
                    self.logs.append({"event": "finish", "step": step})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: finish() called", flush=True)
                    break
                if tool_name == "fail":
                    reason = tool_args.get("reason", "no reason given")
                    self.logs.append({"event": "fail", "step": step, "reason": reason})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: fail({reason})", flush=True)
                    break

                # Inject project_id for artifact tools that accept it
                tool_args["project_id"] = project_id

                try:
                    self.metrics["tool_calls"] += 1
                    _raw = await mcp.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)

                    if hasattr(_raw, "structured_content") and _raw.structured_content is not None:
                        observation = _raw.structured_content
                    elif hasattr(_raw, "content") and _raw.content:
                        texts = [c.text for c in _raw.content if hasattr(c, "text")]
                        try:
                            observation = json.loads(texts[0]) if texts else {}
                        except Exception:
                            observation = texts[0] if texts else str(_raw)
                    else:
                        observation = str(_raw)

                    success = observation.get("success", True) if isinstance(observation, dict) else True
                    if not success:
                        self.metrics["tool_failures"] += 1

                    self.logs.append({
                        "event": "tool_call", "step": step, "tool": tool_name,
                        "args_preview": json.dumps(tool_args)[:300],
                        "success": success,
                        "observation_preview": json.dumps(observation, default=str)[:600],
                    })
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    obs_preview = json.dumps(observation, default=str)
                    if len(obs_preview) > 200:
                        obs_preview = obs_preview[:200] + "..."
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: {tool_name} → success={success} | Result: {obs_preview}", flush=True)
                    tool_history.append({"tool": tool_name, "args": tool_args, "result": observation, "success": success})

                except Exception as exc:
                    self.metrics["tool_failures"] += 1
                    err_msg = str(exc)
                    self.logs.append({
                        "event": "tool_error", "step": step, "tool": tool_name,
                        "error": err_msg, "error_class": classify_error(err_msg),
                    })
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: {tool_name} ERROR: {err_msg[:100]}", flush=True)
                    tool_history.append({"tool": tool_name, "args": tool_args, "result": {"error": err_msg}, "success": False})

"""
Baseline 2: ReAct Agent baseline.

Models the real WorkerAgent loop: JSON-only output format, system+user conversation
turns, explicit tool documentation, finish()/fail() terminators.  The only
difference from a real worker is that this agent creates ALL artifacts for the
full exercise (no subsystem partitioning, no spawn_worker delegation).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import re
import time
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Type

from fastmcp import Client
from fastmcp.client.transports import StreamableHttpTransport
import litellm

import sys
from pathlib import Path
CURRENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent.parent
if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))

from evaluation.baselines import (
    resolve_baseline_model,
    classify_error,
    get_litellm_kwargs,
    trim_messages_to_token_budget,
    build_compact_schema_text,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

MAX_STEPS = 1000
MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 15

# context window management
CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD = 18  # compress when history exceeds this many steps
CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT = 8          # always keep this many recent steps in full detail


class BaselineReActAgent:
    def __init__(
        self,
        models: Dict[str, Type[Any]],
        mcp_server_url: str = "http://localhost:12013/mcp",
        token: str = "",
        topology_yaml: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.models = models
        self.mcp_server_url = mcp_server_url
        self.token = token
        self.topology_yaml = topology_yaml
        self.metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "total_tokens": 0, "prompt_tokens": 0, "completion_tokens": 0,
            "tool_calls": 0, "tool_failures": 0, "llm_calls": 0, "iterations": 0, "duration": 0,
        }
        self.compressed_history_summary = ""
        self.logs: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # LLM CALL
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def _call_llm(self, messages: List[Dict[str, str]]) -> str:
        import asyncio as _asyncio
        self.metrics["llm_calls"] += 1
        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()

        # Log prompt preview to console
        print(f"\n      [RE-ACT STEP {self.metrics['llm_calls']}] Sending prompt to {model_name}...", flush=True)
        # Show only recent messages to avoid cluttering console
        recent = messages[-2:] if len(messages) >= 2 else messages
        for msg in recent:
            preview = (msg['content'][:300] + "...") if len(msg['content']) > 300 else msg['content']
            print(f"      --- {msg['role'].upper()} ---\n      {preview}", flush=True)

        for _attempt in range(5):
            try:
                safe_messages = trim_messages_to_token_budget(messages)
                response = await litellm.acompletion(
                    model=model_name,
                    messages=safe_messages,
                    temperature=0,
                    **get_litellm_kwargs(model_name),
                )
                usage = response.get("usage", {})
                self.metrics["prompt_tokens"] += usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
                self.metrics["completion_tokens"] += usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
                self.metrics["total_tokens"] += usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
                
                content = response.choices[0].message.content
                print(f"      --- ASSISTANT RESPONSE ---\n      {content[:400]}...", flush=True)
                return content
            except Exception as _exc:
                if _attempt < 4 and "too many concurrent" in str(_exc).lower():
                    wait = 30 * (2 ** _attempt)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] Ollama busy, retrying in {wait}s…", flush=True)
                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
                else:
                    raise
        raise RuntimeError("LLM call failed after 5 attempts")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PROMPT CONSTRUCTION
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _build_system_prompt(self, description: str, project_id: str) -> str:
        schema_text = build_compact_schema_text(self.models, self.topology_yaml or "")

        return f"""You are a Worker Agent executing a cyber-exercise creation task.
You must create ALL artifacts required to fully implement the exercise described below.

YOUR ASSIGNMENT:
{description}

PROJECT ID (injected automatically — do NOT pass it as a tool argument):
{project_id}

{schema_text}

FIELD POPULATION RULES:
- Fields marked with * are REQUIRED — you must provide a value.
- REFERENCE fields (marked with "-> TargetClass") point to other artifacts.
  Use set_reference or append_to_collection for these — NEVER put them in batch_set_fields.
- Enum fields must use one of the listed values.
- Populate ALL scalar fields (name, description, ip, cidr, etc.) via batch_set_fields.
- Create artifacts following the phase order (phase 0 first, then phase 1, etc.).

AVAILABLE TOOLS:

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  🔧 ARTIFACT MANAGEMENT TOOLS
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  query_artifacts(model_name)
    → List existing artifacts of a type.
    → Returns: [{{"artifact_id": "...", "data": {{...}}}}]

  get_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Inspect an existing artifact.
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact": {{...}}}}

  get_model_schema(model_name)
    → Get detailed field information for a model (use to discover field names).
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "schema": {{...}}}}

  create_artifact(model_name, common_name?)
    → Create a new empty artifact shell.
    → Parameters: model_name (string), common_name (optional short label)
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact_id": "<UUID>"}}
    → ⚠️  The returned artifact_id UUID is what you use in ALL subsequent calls.

  batch_set_fields(artifact_id, fields_json)
    → Set SCALAR (primitive) fields: strings, numbers, booleans, enums.
    → Parameters:
        • artifact_id: UUID from create_artifact
        • fields_json: JSON string with field→value pairs (scalars only)
    → ❌ NEVER put reference fields here. Reference fields (pointing to other
         artifacts) MUST use set_reference or append_to_collection.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}} or {{"success": false, "error": "..."}}

  set_reference(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
    → Link a single-valued reference field to another artifact.
    → Parameters:
        • artifact_id: UUID of the artifact being modified
        • field_name: the reference field name (from get_model_schema)
        • reference_value: UUID of the artifact to reference
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  append_to_collection(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
    → Append to a list/collection reference field.
    → Parameters same as set_reference.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  validate_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Validate against the metamodel schema.
    → Returns: {{"valid": true}} or {{"valid": false, "errors": [...]}}

  commit_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Finalize the artifact. Call ONLY after validate returns valid=true.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  ✅ COMPLETION SIGNALS
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  finish()
    → Signal that ALL artifacts are created, validated, and committed.
    → Call this as your LAST action when the full exercise is done.

  fail(reason)
    → Signal that you cannot complete the task. Use only if truly stuck.

RULES:
  1. Use get_model_schema(model_name) to discover field names and types before
     creating an artifact if you are unsure of its fields.
  2. Call create_artifact once per artifact to get its UUID.
  3. Call batch_set_fields with ALL scalar fields in a single call per artifact.
  4. Use set_reference / append_to_collection for any field that points to another
     artifact — NEVER pass artifact data as a dict inside batch_set_fields.
  5. Call validate_artifact before commit_artifact.
  6. Fix validation errors before committing.
  7. Call commit_artifact only after validation passes.
  8. When all artifacts are committed, call finish().
  9. NEVER repeat a tool call with the exact same arguments — reuse results from history.
  10. Do NOT pass project_id as a tool argument — it is injected automatically.
  11. Build artifacts bottom-up: create dependencies (Networks, Hardware) before
      the artifacts that reference them (Nodes, VMs).

OUTPUT FORMAT — respond with ONLY valid JSON, no prose, no markdown:
{{"tool": "tool_name_here", "args": {{"arg1": "value1"}}}}

Examples:
  {{"tool": "get_model_schema", "args": {{"model_name": "Node"}}}}
  {{"tool": "create_artifact", "args": {{"model_name": "Network", "common_name": "hq_lan"}}}}
  {{"tool": "batch_set_fields", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "fields_json": "{{\\"name\\": \\"HQ LAN\\", \\"cidr\\": \\"10.0.0.0/24\\"}}"}}}}
  {{"tool": "set_reference", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "field_name": "topology", "reference_value": "<topology_uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "validate_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "commit_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "finish", "args": {{}}}}
"""

    def _maybe_compress_history(self, tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Rolling compression of tool history to stay within context limits."""
        if len(tool_history) <= CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD:
            return tool_history

        to_compress = tool_history[:-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT]
        remaining = tool_history[-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT:]

        lines: List[str] = []
        create_ids: List[str] = []
        fields_set: List[str] = []
        validations_passed = 0
        failures = 0

        for entry in to_compress:
            tool_name = entry.get("tool")
            res = entry.get("result", {})
            success = entry.get("success", True)
            
            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and success:
                aid = res.get("artifact_id", "?")
                create_ids.append(aid)
            elif tool_name == "batch_set_fields" and success:
                try:
                    fkeys = list(json.loads(entry.get("args", {}).get("fields_json", "{}")).keys())
                    fields_set.extend(fkeys)
                except Exception:
                    pass
            elif tool_name == "validate_artifact":
                if res.get("valid"):
                    validations_passed += 1
            if not success:
                failures += 1

        if create_ids:
            lines.append(f"Created artifact(s): {', '.join(create_ids)}")
        if fields_set:
            unique_fields = list(dict.fromkeys(fields_set))
            lines.append(f"Fields set: {', '.join(unique_fields[:20])}")
        if validations_passed:
            lines.append(f"Validation passed {validations_passed} time(s)")
        if failures:
            lines.append(f"Tool failures encountered: {failures}")

        summary_chunk = f"[Steps 1-{len(to_compress)} compressed] " + "; ".join(lines) + "."
        if self.compressed_history_summary:
            self.compressed_history_summary += "\n" + summary_chunk
        else:
            self.compressed_history_summary = summary_chunk
            
        return remaining

    def _build_messages(
        self,
        system_prompt: str,
        tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]],
        consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
        messages: List[Dict[str, str]] = [{"role": "system", "content": system_prompt}]

        if self.compressed_history_summary:
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"PROGRESS SUMMARY (earlier steps):\n{self.compressed_history_summary}"})

        for entry in tool_history:
            messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": json.dumps({"tool": entry["tool"], "args": entry["args"]})})
            obs = json.dumps(entry["result"], default=str)
            if len(obs) > 4000:
                obs = obs[:4000] + "… [truncated]"
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"OBSERVATION: {obs}"})

        if len(consecutive_failures) >= 2:
            error_lines = "\n".join(
                f"  • {e['tool']}: {e['result'].get('error', 'failed')}"
                for e in consecutive_failures
            )
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
                f"⚠️  REPEATED TOOL FAILURES ({len(consecutive_failures)} in a row):\n"
                f"{error_lines}\n\n"
                "You must change your approach. Before your next action:\n"
                "1. State the ROOT CAUSE of these failures\n"
                "2. Describe your CORRECTED PLAN\n"
                "3. Then provide the corrected JSON action"
            )})
        elif len(consecutive_failures) == 1:
            e = consecutive_failures[0]
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
                f"The previous {e['tool']} call failed: {e['result'].get('error', 'unknown error')}\n"
                "Briefly reason about what went wrong, then provide a corrected JSON action."
            )})

        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": "What is your next action? Respond with JSON only."})
        return messages

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # JSON PARSER (same balanced-brace approach as WorkerAgent)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _parse_action(self, content: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        content = content.strip()
        try:
            return json.loads(content)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            pass
        code_block = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```", content)
        if code_block:
            try:
                return json.loads(code_block.group(1))
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                pass
        for start in range(len(content)):
            if content[start] != "{":
                continue
            depth, in_string, escape_next = 0, False, False
            for end in range(start, len(content)):
                ch = content[end]
                if escape_next:
                    escape_next = False
                    continue
                if ch == "\\" and in_string:
                    escape_next = True
                    continue
                if ch == '"':
                    in_string = not in_string
                    continue
                if in_string:
                    continue
                if ch == "{":
                    depth += 1
                elif ch == "}":
                    depth -= 1
                    if depth == 0:
                        try:
                            return json.loads(content[start:end + 1])
                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
                            break
        return None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # MAIN LOOP
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def generate(
        self, description: str, project_id: str = "", log_callback: Optional[callable] = None
    ) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()

        self.logs.append({
            "event": "start",
            "model": model_name,
            "mcp_url": self.mcp_server_url,
            "project_id": project_id,
            "description_preview": description[:200],
        })
        if log_callback:
            log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)

        transport = StreamableHttpTransport(url=self.mcp_server_url)
        async with Client(transport=transport) as mcp:
            mcp_tools = await mcp.list_tools()
            tool_names = [t.name for t in mcp_tools]
            self.logs.append({"event": "tools_discovered", "tools": tool_names})
            if log_callback:
                log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
            print(f"      [react_agent] discovered {len(tool_names)} MCP tools", flush=True)

            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(description, project_id)
            tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

            for step in range(MAX_STEPS):
                self.metrics["iterations"] += 1
                
                # Compress history if it exceeds threshold
                tool_history = self._maybe_compress_history(tool_history)

                # Compute consecutive tail failures for error injection
                consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
                for entry in reversed(tool_history):
                    if not entry.get("success", True):
                        consecutive_failures.insert(0, entry)
                    else:
                        break

                if len(consecutive_failures) >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES:
                    self.logs.append({"event": "fatal_failures", "step": step, "count": len(consecutive_failures)})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: EXCEEDED {MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES} CONSECUTIVE FAILURES. Stopping.", flush=True)
                    break

                messages = self._build_messages(system_prompt, tool_history, consecutive_failures)
                raw = await self._call_llm(messages)
                action = self._parse_action(raw)

                if action is None:
                    self.logs.append({"event": "parse_failure", "step": step, "raw_preview": raw[:300]})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: could not parse JSON action", flush=True)
                    # Inject parse failure feedback and continue
                    tool_history.append({
                        "tool": "_parse_error",
                        "args": {},
                        "result": {"error": f"Your response was not valid JSON. Raw: {raw[:200]}"},
                        "success": False,
                    })
                    continue

                tool_name = action.get("tool", "")
                tool_args = action.get("args", {})

                # Termination signals
                if tool_name == "finish":
                    self.logs.append({"event": "finish", "step": step})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: finish() called", flush=True)
                    break
                if tool_name == "fail":
                    reason = tool_args.get("reason", "no reason given")
                    self.logs.append({"event": "fail", "step": step, "reason": reason})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: fail({reason})", flush=True)
                    break

                # Inject project_id for artifact tools that accept it
                tool_args["project_id"] = project_id

                try:
                    self.metrics["tool_calls"] += 1
                    _raw = await mcp.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)

                    if hasattr(_raw, "structured_content") and _raw.structured_content is not None:
                        observation = _raw.structured_content
                    elif hasattr(_raw, "content") and _raw.content:
                        texts = [c.text for c in _raw.content if hasattr(c, "text")]
                        try:
                            observation = json.loads(texts[0]) if texts else {}
                        except Exception:
                            observation = texts[0] if texts else str(_raw)
                    else:
                        observation = str(_raw)

                    success = observation.get("success", True) if isinstance(observation, dict) else True
                    if not success:
                        self.metrics["tool_failures"] += 1

                    self.logs.append({
                        "event": "tool_call", "step": step, "tool": tool_name,
                        "args_preview": json.dumps(tool_args)[:300],
                        "success": success,
                        "observation_preview": json.dumps(observation, default=str)[:600],
                    })
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    obs_preview = json.dumps(observation, default=str)
                    if len(obs_preview) > 200:
                        obs_preview = obs_preview[:200] + "..."
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: {tool_name} → success={success} | Result: {obs_preview}", flush=True)
                    tool_history.append({"tool": tool_name, "args": tool_args, "result": observation, "success": success})

                except Exception as exc:
                    self.metrics["tool_failures"] += 1
                    err_msg = str(exc)
                    self.logs.append({
                        "event": "tool_error", "step": step, "tool": tool_name,
                        "error": err_msg, "error_class": classify_error(err_msg),
                    })
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: {tool_name} ERROR: {err_msg[:100]}", flush=True)
                    tool_history.append({"tool": tool_name, "args": tool_args, "result": {"error": err_msg}, "success": False})

displayContent.text:
"""
Baseline 3: Code-Generation Agent.

The LLM is asked to write Python code that instantiates the Pydantic metamodel
classes for a given scenario description. The generated code is executed locally,
and the resulting artifact instances are serialised to dicts and returned directly
(no DB persistence — validation happens offline from the JSON output).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import time
import traceback
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Tuple, Type, Optional

import litellm

import sys
from pathlib import Path

CURRENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent.parent
if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))
ORCHESTRATOR_DIR = SRC_DIR / "langgraph-orchestrator"
if str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR))

from evaluation.baselines import (
    resolve_baseline_model,
    classify_error,
    get_litellm_kwargs,
    trim_messages_to_token_budget,
    build_compact_schema_text,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_SYSTEM_TEMPLATE = """\
You are an expert Python engineer who creates cyber-range infrastructure models.
You will be given a scenario description and a set of Pydantic model schemas.

IMPORTANT: The following metamodel classes are ALREADY available as Python variables
in your execution scope — do NOT import or redefine them:
  {class_names}

Your task is to write **executable Python code** that:
  1. Imports ONLY standard library modules if needed (uuid, datetime, etc.) — no third-party imports.
  2. Uses the already-available metamodel classes listed above to create instances.
  3. Stores every created instance in a top-level dict called `artifacts` with
     the exact format:
         artifacts = {{
             "ModelName": [instance.model_dump(), ...],
             ...
         }}
     where each value is a list of serialised dicts (call `.model_dump()` on
     each instance before appending).

RULES:
- NEVER redefine or subclass the metamodel classes — use them as-is.
- Use ONLY the class names listed above — do not invent new ones.
- Every field value must satisfy the JSON schema (correct types, required fields).
- Assign `id` or `name` fields so cross-references are possible.
- REFERENCE fields (marked with "-> TargetClass") must be set to the `id`/`uuid`
  string of the target instance. Create dependencies BEFORE the instances that
  reference them (follow the phase order in the schema).
- Populate ALL fields marked with * (required). Fill optional fields too when
  the scenario provides relevant information.
- Do NOT include any print statements or I/O.
- Return ONLY the Python code block — no prose outside the code fence.

{schema_text}
"""

_USER_TEMPLATE = "Scenario description:\n{description}\n\nWrite the Python code now."

_RETRY_MESSAGE = "The previous code had an error:\n{error}\n\nFix the code and try again. Return ONLY the corrected Python code block."


class BaselineCodeGen:
    """Code-generation baseline: LLM writes Python, we exec() it, then persist."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        models: Dict[str, Type[Any]],
        mcp_server_url: str = "http://localhost:12013/mcp",
        model_name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
        topology_yaml: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.models = models
        self.mcp_server_url = mcp_server_url
        self.model_name = model_name or resolve_baseline_model()
        self.max_retries = max_retries
        self.topology_yaml = topology_yaml
        self.metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "total_tokens": 0, "prompt_tokens": 0, "completion_tokens": 0,
            "llm_calls": 0, "retries": 0, "exec_errors": 0,
            "artifacts_created": 0, "duration": 0,
        }
        self.logs: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def _call_llm(self, messages: List[Dict[str, str]]) -> Tuple[str, Any]:
        import asyncio as _asyncio
        self.metrics["llm_calls"] += 1
        
        # Log prompt preview to console
        print(f"\n      [CODE-GEN LLM CALL {self.metrics['llm_calls']}] Sending prompt to {self.model_name}...", flush=True)
        for msg in messages:
            preview = (msg['content'][:300] + "...") if len(msg['content']) > 300 else msg['content']
            print(f"      --- {msg['role'].upper()} ---\n      {preview}", flush=True)

        for _attempt in range(5):
            try:
                safe_messages = trim_messages_to_token_budget(messages)
                response = await litellm.acompletion(
                    model=self.model_name,
                    messages=safe_messages,
                    temperature=0,
                    **get_litellm_kwargs(self.model_name),
                )
                break
            except Exception as _exc:
                if _attempt < 4 and "too many concurrent" in str(_exc).lower():
                    wait = 30 * (2 ** _attempt)
                    print(f"      [code_gen] Ollama busy, retrying in {wait}s…", flush=True)
                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
                else:
                    raise
        usage = response.get("usage", {})
        self.metrics["prompt_tokens"] += usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
        self.metrics["completion_tokens"] += usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
        self.metrics["total_tokens"] += usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
        
        content = response.choices[0].message.content
        print(f"      --- ASSISTANT RESPONSE ---\n      {content[:500]}...", flush=True)
        return content, response

    def _get_schema_text(self) -> str:
        return build_compact_schema_text(self.models, self.topology_yaml or "")

    def _extract_code(self, text: str) -> str:
        m = re.search(r"```(?:python)?\s*\n([\s\S]*?)```", text)
        if m:
            return m.group(1).strip()
        return text.strip()

    def _exec_code(self, code: str) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict[str, List[Dict]]], Optional[str]]:
        namespace: Dict[str, Any] = {"__builtins__": __builtins__}
        namespace.update(self.models)
        try:
            exec(compile(code, "<generated>", "exec"), namespace)  # noqa: S102
        except Exception:
            return None, traceback.format_exc()

        raw = namespace.get("artifacts")
        if raw is None:
            return None, "Code executed but `artifacts` variable was not defined."
        if not isinstance(raw, dict):
            return None, f"`artifacts` must be a dict, got {type(raw).__name__}."

        normalised: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {}
        for key, value in raw.items():
            if not isinstance(value, list):
                value = [value]
            dicts = []
            for item in value:
                if isinstance(item, dict):
                    dicts.append(item)
                elif hasattr(item, "model_dump"):
                    dicts.append(item.model_dump())
                else:
                    dicts.append(dict(item))
            normalised[key] = dicts
        return normalised, None

    async def generate(
        self, description: str, project_id: str = "", log_callback: Optional[callable] = None
    ) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
        start = time.perf_counter()

        self.logs.append({
            "event": "start",
            "model": self.model_name,
            "mcp_url": self.mcp_server_url,
            "project_id": project_id,
            "description_preview": description[:200],
        })
        if log_callback:
            log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)

        schema_text = self._get_schema_text()
        class_names = ", ".join(self.models.keys())

        system_prompt = _SYSTEM_TEMPLATE.format(
            schema_text=schema_text,
            class_names=class_names
        )

        messages: List[Dict[str, str]] = [
            {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
            {"role": "user", "content": _USER_TEMPLATE.format(description=description)},
        ]
        artifacts: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {}

        for attempt in range(self.max_retries + 1):
            raw_response, _ = await self._call_llm(messages)
            code = self._extract_code(raw_response)
            result, error = self._exec_code(code)

            self.logs.append({
                "event": "codegen_attempt",
                "attempt": attempt,
                "code_length": len(code),
                "code_preview": code[:600],
                "exec_ok": error is None,
                "error": error[:500] if error else None,
                "error_class": classify_error(error) if error else None,
                "token_totals": {"prompt": self.metrics["prompt_tokens"], "completion": self.metrics["completion_tokens"]},
            })
            if log_callback:
                log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
            print(f"      [code_gen] attempt {attempt}: exec_ok={error is None}, tokens={self.metrics['total_tokens']}", flush=True)

            if error:
                self.metrics["exec_errors"] += 1
                if attempt < self.max_retries:
                    self.metrics["retries"] += 1
                    messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": raw_response})
                    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": _RETRY_MESSAGE.format(error=error[:800])})
                continue

            artifacts = result or {}
            break

        if artifacts:
            counts = {k: len(v) for k, v in artifacts.items()}
            self.metrics["artifacts_created"] = sum(counts.values())
            self.logs.append({
                "event": "artifacts_produced",
                "artifact_counts_by_model": counts,
                "total_artifacts": sum(counts.values()),
                "artifacts_data": artifacts,
            })
            print(f"      [code_gen] produced {sum(counts.values())} artifacts: {counts}")
        else:
            self.logs.append({"event": "artifacts_produced", "skipped": True, "reason": "no valid artifacts produced after all attempts"})
            print("      [code_gen] no valid artifacts produced")

        self.metrics["duration"] = time.perf_counter() - start
        self.logs.append({"event": "done", "metrics": dict(self.metrics)})
        return artifacts, self.logs

                            json.dumps({"steps": current_logs, "metrics": current_metrics}, indent=2, default=str),
                            encoding="utf-8",
                        )
                        _fix_ownership(log_path)

                    # baseline_artifacts is set by JSON-only baselines (no DB persistence)

                    if special_type == "direct_llm":
                        from evaluation.baselines.baseline_direct_llm import BaselineDirectLLM
                        bl = BaselineDirectLLM(

                            mcp_server_url=_mcp_url,
                            topology_yaml=self.metamodel_topology_yaml
                        )
                        baseline_artifacts, step_logs = await bl.generate(_task_prompt, project_id=project_id, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = bl.metrics
                    elif special_type == "react_agent":
                        from evaluation.baselines.baseline_react_agent import BaselineReActAgent
                        bl = BaselineReActAgent(
                            self.metamodel_models,
                            mcp_server_url=_mcp_url,
                            token=self.token,
                            topology_yaml=self.metamodel_topology_yaml
                        )
                        _, step_logs = await bl.generate(_task_prompt, project_id=project_id, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = bl.metrics

                        bl = BaselineCodeGen(

                            mcp_server_url=_mcp_url,
                            topology_yaml=self.metamodel_topology_yaml
                        )
                        baseline_artifacts, step_logs = await bl.generate(_task_prompt, project_id=project_id, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = bl.metrics
                    else:
                        events: List[Any] = []
                        await runner._run_project_and_capture_events(client, exercise, project_id, events, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = {"tool_calls": len(events)}
                        step_logs = events
                        # Final write (redundant but safe)
                        _write_incremental_logs(step_logs, metrics)

                    # Save artifact snapshot for offline reproducibility.
                    # JSON-only baselines (direct_llm, code_gen) never touch the DB,
                    # so we save their artifacts directly. react_agent and the full
                    # system persist to DB and use the REST API fetch path.
                    if baseline_artifacts is not None:
                        snapshot_path = self._save_baseline_snapshot(
                            exercise.ID, project_id, baseline_artifacts, logs_dir
                        )
                        artifacts_count = sum(len(v) for v in baseline_artifacts.values())

                    log_path = logs_dir / f"exercise_{exercise.ID}_{project_id}.json"
                    
                    def _write_incremental_logs(current_logs, current_metrics):
                        """Callback to write logs to disk during execution."""
                        log_path.write_text(
                            json.dumps({"steps": current_logs, "metrics": current_metrics}, indent=2, default=str),
                            encoding="utf-8",
                        )
                        _fix_ownership(log_path)

                    # baseline_artifacts is set by JSON-only baselines (no DB persistence)
                    baseline_artifacts: Optional[Dict[str, List[Dict]]] = None

                    if special_type == "direct_llm":
                        from evaluation.baselines.baseline_direct_llm import BaselineDirectLLM
                        bl = BaselineDirectLLM(
                            self.metamodel_models,
                            mcp_server_url=_mcp_url,
                            topology_yaml=self.metamodel_topology_yaml
                        )
                        baseline_artifacts, step_logs = await bl.generate(_task_prompt, project_id=project_id, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = bl.metrics
                    elif special_type == "react_agent":
                        from evaluation.baselines.baseline_react_agent import BaselineReActAgent
                        bl = BaselineReActAgent(
                            self.metamodel_models,
                            mcp_server_url=_mcp_url,
                            token=self.token,
                            topology_yaml=self.metamodel_topology_yaml
                        )
                        _, step_logs = await bl.generate(_task_prompt, project_id=project_id, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = bl.metrics
                    elif special_type == "code_gen":
                        from evaluation.baselines.baseline_code_gen import BaselineCodeGen
                        bl = BaselineCodeGen(
                            self.metamodel_models,
                            mcp_server_url=_mcp_url,
                            topology_yaml=self.metamodel_topology_yaml
                        )
                        baseline_artifacts, step_logs = await bl.generate(_task_prompt, project_id=project_id, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = bl.metrics
                    else:
                        events: List[Any] = []
                        await runner._run_project_and_capture_events(client, exercise, project_id, events, log_callback=_write_incremental_logs)
                        metrics = {"tool_calls": len(events)}
                        step_logs = events
                        # Final write (redundant but safe)
                        _write_incremental_logs(step_logs, metrics)

                    # Save artifact snapshot for offline reproducibility.
                    # JSON-only baselines (direct_llm, code_gen) never touch the DB,
                    # so we save their artifacts directly. react_agent and the full
                    # system persist to DB and use the REST API fetch path.
                    if baseline_artifacts is not None:
                        snapshot_path = self._save_baseline_snapshot(
                            exercise.ID, project_id, baseline_artifacts, logs_dir
                        )
                        artifacts_count = sum(len(v) for v in baseline_artifacts.values())
